key_insight: The paper treats ammonia-catalyst discovery as a multi-intermediate reaction-network problem and finds that true four-intermediate compatibility only appears at ten-million-scale generative search.
engineering_relevance: This enables more realistic catalyst screening by prioritizing materials that work across competing reaction pathways rather than optimizing a single adsorbate.
method: Adsorbate-specific generative Transformers explore ~15 million configurations per intermediate (N*, NH*, NNH*, HNNH*).
High-throughput machine-learning potentials predict stability/compatibility and compress the candidate set.
Representative DFT checks validate pathway-specific leads for dissociative and associative mechanisms.
evidence_signal: Scale-dependent overlap analysis showed that full four-intermediate compatibility was not captured at 10^5–10^6 scale but emerged only at ten-million-scale exploration, yielding 279 target materials after compression.
watch_item: The workflow is computationally intensive and depends on ML-potential fidelity plus the extent to which generated candidates remain chemically realistic before DFT validation.
paper_title: Reaction-Network-Level Discovery of Ammonia Synthesis Catalysts via Ten-Million-Scale Generative Exploration
